era: Indie Rock/Pop. E=5 -- Seattle's longest-running punk/pop band. Since 1979. Predates Sub Pop, predates grunge. The foundation layer. v1.49.334.

location.formative: Working-class Scandinavian neighborhood. Maritime heritage. Punk-aligned DIY ethos.
location.career: Fastbacks. 1979-2001. PopLlama Records. 22 years of continuous operation.

members.name: Kurt Bloch
members.note: Also in Young Fresh Fellows (degree 192). PopLlama bridge. One of Seattle's most prolific guitarists.

members.name: Kim Warnick
members.note: Early prominent woman in Seattle punk. Melodic bass, bright vocal harmonies.

members.name: Lulu Gargiulo
members.note: Rhythm guitar, vocal harmony pair with Warnick. Dense layered guitar sound.

former_members.name: Duff McKagan
former_members.note: Later Guns N' Roses. Seattle-to-LA connection.

theory_nodes.concept: Foundation layer -- the Fastbacks existed before the Seattle scene. Since 1979, 7 years before Sub Pop, 10 before grunge. They proved original music could survive in Seattle. The pre-scene infrastructure.
examples: 1979: Fastbacks form. 1986: Sub Pop founded. 1989: Nirvana Bleach. 1990: Pearl Jam forms.
Longest-running punk band in Seattle history.

genealogy.relationship: The Fastbacks are the foundation all later Seattle music is built on.

theory_nodes.concept: PopLlama streak -- consecutive degrees 192 (Young Fresh Fellows) and 193 (Fastbacks) on the same label. Kurt Bloch played in both bands. Personnel link, not just label coincidence.
examples: PopLlama founded 1984, predates Sub Pop by 2 years.

genealogy.relationship: Fastbacks at 193 continue PopLlama streak via Kurt Bloch.

theory_nodes.concept: Ballard as punk geography -- working-class, Scandinavian, maritime, blue-collar. Punk's DIY ethic fits Ballard's no-pretension culture. New neighborhood enters the engine.
examples: Not Capitol Hill bohemia or University District art.

genealogy.relationship: Ballard adds a new geographic node to the engine map.

cross_references.relationship: POPLLAMA STREAK! Kurt Bloch in both bands. Consecutive PopLlama degrees. Personnel bridge.
Post-hip-hop indie rock zone. Fastbacks predate Mudhoney by a decade. Foundation to successor.
Power-pop peers. Similar melodic sensibility. The Posies learned from what the Fastbacks built.
Indie rock zone 189-193. The post-hip-hop transition consolidates.
